skill.name: bash-scripting
skill.description: Bash scripting workflow for creating production-ready shell scripts with defensive patterns, error handling, logging, documentation, and testing.
skill.summary: Create safer Bash scripts with structure, defensive patterns, tests, and documentation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many command, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found they are markdown examples, skill names, workflow headings, or shell-template syntax. No malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, exfiltration, or hidden executable payload was found in SKILL.md. The skill still carries medium operational risk because it guides users to create shell automation that may run system commands.

medium_findings.title: Shell Automation Guidance Requires Review
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to help create automation, deployment, backup, and system administration scripts. This is legitimate, but generated Bash can affect files, processes, and host configuration if users run it without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly targets shell automation and system commands, which creates operational risk. I found no evidence that the skill itself executes commands or hides malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Hits Are Markdown References
low_findings.description: Most external command detections are inline skill names or fenced copy-paste prompts in markdown. They do not execute during skill loading and are false positives for command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears in markdown lists or prompt examples, not executable source files. The repository file structure only contains SKILL.md, so there is no hidden script payload in this skill.

low_findings.title: Command Substitution Appears In Example Template
low_findings.description: The shell command substitutions are ordinary Bash template lines for script name, script directory, and timestamp logging. They are safe as documentation, but users should still review generated scripts before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The substitutions are visible inside a fenced Bash template and use common commands such as basename, cd, dirname, pwd, and date. They are not invoked by the marketplace skill itself.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography and network reconnaissance locations contain metadata, workflow text, action labels, prompts, and a Bats testing checklist item. No cryptographic algorithm or network probing command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, nmap, netcat, port scanning, or similar behavior at the flagged lines. The detections are caused by text matching rather than semantic risk.

content.value_statement: Shell scripts often fail because they lack input validation, error handling, and repeatable tests. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a structured Bash workflow for safer automation.
actual_capabilities: Plans Bash script purpose, inputs, outputs, and error handling before implementation.
Provides a phased workflow for structure, logging, testing, and documentation.
Prompts use of defensive Bash patterns such as strict mode and cleanup handlers.
Includes a reusable script template with usage help and logging functions.
Defines quality gates for ShellCheck, Bats tests, error handling, and documentation.
limitations: It is a workflow document, not a runnable script generator by itself.
It references related skills that may not be installed in every environment.
Generated scripts still require human review before execution on real systems.
It does not include full Bats test files or ShellCheck configuration files.

use_cases.title: Create Operations Automation
use_cases.description: Build Bash scripts for routine system administration with clear inputs, logs, and failure handling.

use_cases.title: Prepare Deployment Helpers
use_cases.description: Design deployment scripts that document dependencies, handle errors, and expose readable usage information.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Shell Scripts
use_cases.description: Review an existing script against defensive structure, logging, tests, and documentation quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Bash Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the bash-scripting workflow to plan a Bash script for this task: [describe task]. Identify inputs, outputs, risks, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Defensive Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the bash-scripting workflow to draft a Bash script for [task]. Include strict mode, usage help, validation, logging, and cleanup handling.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the bash-scripting workflow to review this Bash script for reliability, safety, ShellCheck issues, missing tests, and unclear documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Tested Automation Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the full bash-scripting workflow to design a production-ready automation script for [system task]. Include rollout risks, test cases, rollback behavior, and documentation.

output_examples.input: Create a backup script for a project directory.
output: A script plan with expected arguments, destination rules, and failure cases.
A Bash draft that includes strict mode, logging, usage help, and cleanup.
A test checklist covering missing directories, permission errors, and repeated runs.

output_examples.input: Review my deployment shell script before release.
output: A prioritized review of unsafe assumptions, missing validation, and unclear exits.
Suggested improvements for logging, rollback behavior, and dependency checks.
A quality gate list for ShellCheck, Bats tests, and documentation.

best_practices: Review every generated command before running it on a real host.
Run ShellCheck and focused tests before using scripts in production.
Keep destructive operations guarded by validation, dry-run modes, or explicit confirmation.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated scripts with elevated privileges before review.
Do not hide errors by disabling strict mode without a clear reason.
Do not embed secrets, tokens, or passwords directly in shell scripts.

faq.question: Does this skill execute shell commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a markdown workflow that helps an AI assistant design or review Bash scripts.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The file is not malicious, but Bash automation can modify systems when users run generated scripts.

faq.question: Does it include complete tests?
faq.answer: No. It recommends Bats and ShellCheck, but users must create or request concrete test files.

faq.question: What kinds of scripts is it best for?
faq.answer: It fits automation, deployment helpers, backup scripts, CI tasks, and system administration tools.

faq.question: What should I check before running generated Bash?
faq.answer: Check commands, paths, permissions, error handling, cleanup behavior, and handling of secrets.
